tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

reply_to_id.description: Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments ŌĆö ignored for issue and pull request comments.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required ŌĆö omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) ŌĆö it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58364,"title":"[AgentServer] Docs: document eager previous_response_id validation and ResourceNotFoundException.Code/Param","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** PR #58347 by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed Beta.3 introduced two significant behavioral changes: 1. **Eager validation of `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id`** ŌĆö the library now resolves these references against the storage provider *before* invoking the handler. Invalid `previous_response_id` returns HTTP 404 immediately; invalid `conversation.id` returns HTTP 400. Previously, these errors were silently ignored. 2. **Richer storage error propagation** ŌĆö `ResourceNotFoundException` gained two new public properties, `Code` and `Param`, mirroring `BadRequestException`. `StorageErrorMapper` now extracts the full Foundry error body (`code`, `message`, `param`, `type`) and propagates it through the exception hierarchy. Non-400/404/409 upstream errors are normalized to HTTP 500 instead of being proxied as-is. ## Gaps Found - The **Troubleshooting** section documents the 404 case only as "The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL." It must also document the new 404 scenario: invalid `previous_response_id` detected during eager validation, and the matching 400 for an invalid `conversation.id`. - The **`ResponseContext`** description for `GetHistoryAsync()` does not mention that history/conversation ID resolution now happens eagerly before the handler is invoked, so handlers will never see a lazy 404 mid-stream from those calls. This is important for handler authors to understand. - There is no documentation (in the README or in `docs/handler-implementation-guide.md`) explaining the new `Code` and `Param` properties on `ResourceNotFoundException`, nor how provider implementers should populate them when throwing this exception. - The README has no mention of the new guarantee that non-4xx upstream storage errors are normalized to HTTP 500, which affects how operators reason about error codes surfaced to clients. ­¤ōÉ Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` ŌĆö Edit **A. Update the `ResponseContext` bullet for `GetHistoryAsync`** Locate the bullet: ``` - **`GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken)`** ŌĆö returns output items from previous responses in the conversation chain (oldest-first). Uses `previous_response_id` to walk the conversation and resolves items via the provider. Limit controlled by `ResponsesServerOptions.DefaultFetchHistoryCount` (default: 100). ``` Replace with: ``` - **`GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken)`** ŌĆö returns output items from previous responses in the conversation chain (oldest-first). Uses `previous_response_id` to walk the conversation and resolves items via the provider. Limit controlled by `ResponsesServerOptions.DefaultFetchHistoryCount` (default: 100). The library validates `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` against the provider *before* invoking the handler ŌĆö invalid references return HTTP 404 or HTTP 400 immediately, and the resolved result is cached so handlers calling `GetHistoryAsync()` reuse it without a second storage lookup. ``` **B. Update the Troubleshooting section** Locate the `### Common errors` section and add two new bullets after the existing 404 entry: ```markdown - **404 Not Found** (create): The `previous_response_id` field in the request does not match any existing response. Validation occurs before the handler is invoked. - **400 Bad Request** (create): The `conversation.id` field in the request is invalid or references an unknown conversation. Validation occurs before the handler is invoked. ``` Also update the existing entry: ``` - **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL. ``` to: ``` - **404 Not Found** (get/cancel/delete): The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL. ``` **C. Add a note about upstream error normalization in Troubleshooting** After the existing common errors list, add: ```markdown ### Storage error propagation When the library calls Foundry storage and receives a structured error response, it forwards the full error body ŌĆö `code`, `message`, `param`, and `type` ŌĆö to the client for 400, 404, and 409 responses. All other upstream error status codes are normalized to HTTP 500 to prevent leaking infrastructure details. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` ŌĆö Edit Find the section that describes exception types thrown from provider implementations (if present). Add documentation for the updated `ResourceNotFoundException`: ```markdown #### ResourceNotFoundException Throw `ResourceNotFoundException` from a provider method when a requested resource (response, input item, etc.) does not exist. The library maps this to HTTP 404. ``` throw new ResourceNotFoundException( message: "Response not found.", code: "not_found", // Optional: structured error code forwarded to the client param: "response_id"); // Optional: the field name that caused the error ``` The `Code` and `Param` properties (added in 1.0.0-beta.3) are forwarded verbatim in the HTTP error response body, mirroring the `BadRequestException` API. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet test files are required for these changes ŌĆö the Troubleshooting and provider exception guidance does not need live code samples. Existing snippets remain valid. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections in order: 1. Getting started Ō£ģ (Install, Prerequisites, Configure the server) 2. Key concepts Ō£ģ 3. Examples Ō£ģ 4. Troubleshooting Ō£ģ ŌĆö needs the additions described in Step 1 5. Next steps Ō£ģ 6. Contributing Ō£ģ No structural changes are needed; only the content additions described in Step 1. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` ŌĆö confirm no README changes (no new snippet regions were added) 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` ŌĆö no public API changes are expected for these doc edits 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Firewall blocked 2 domains > > The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `pkgs.dev.azure.com` > - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "pkgs.dev.azure.com" > - "releaseassets.githubusercontent.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24653631953/agentic_workflow) ┬Ę ŌŚÅ 342K ┬Ę [ŌŚĘ](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58364","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:57Z","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:57Z"}

Update the Troubleshooting section** Locate the `### Common errors` section and add two new bullets after the existing 404 entry: ```markdown - **404 Not Found** (create): The `previous_response_id` field in the request does not match any existing response. Validation occurs before the handler is invoked. - **400 Bad Request** (create): The `conversation.id` field in the request is invalid or references an unknown conversation. Validation occurs before the handler is invoked. ``` Also update the existing entry: ``` - **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL. ``` to: ``` - **404 Not Found** (get/cancel/delete): The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL. ``` **C. All other upstream error status codes are normalized to HTTP 500 to prevent leaking infrastructure details. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` ŌĆö Edit Find the section that describes exception types thrown from provider implementations (if present). The library maps this to HTTP 404. ``` throw new ResourceNotFoundException( message: "Response not found.", code: "not_found", // Optional: structured error code forwarded to the client param: "response_id"); // Optional: the field name that caused the error ``` The `Code` and `Param` properties (added in 1.0.0-beta.3) are forwarded verbatim in the HTTP error response body, mirroring the `BadRequestException` API. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet test files are required for these changes ŌĆö the Troubleshooting and provider exception guidance does not need live code samples. Update the Troubleshooting section** Locate the `### Common errors` section and add two new bullets after the existing 404 entry: ```markdown - **404 Not Found** (create): The `previous_response_id` field in the request does not match any existing response. Validation occurs before the handler is invoked. - **400 Bad Request** (create): The `conversation.id` field in the request is invalid or references an unknown conversation. Validation occurs before the handler is invoked. ``` Also update the existing entry: ``` - **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL. ``` to: ``` - **404 Not Found** (get/cancel/delete): The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL. ``` **C. Add a note about upstream error normalization in Troubleshooting** After the existing common errors list, add: ```markdown ### Storage error propagation When the library calls Foundry storage and receives a structured error response, it forwards the full error body ŌĆö `code`, `message`, `param`, and `type` ŌĆö to the client for 400, 404, and 409 responses. All other upstream error status codes are normalized to HTTP 500 to prevent leaking infrastructure details. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` ŌĆö Edit Find the section that describes exception types thrown from provider implementations (if present). Add documentation for the updated `ResourceNotFoundException`: ```markdown #### ResourceNotFoundException Throw `ResourceNotFoundException` from a provider method when a requested resource (response, input item, etc.) does not exist. The library maps this to HTTP 404. ``` throw new ResourceNotFoundException( message: "Response not found.", code: "not_found", // Optional: structured error code forwarded to the client param: "response_id"); // Optional: the field name that caused the error ``` The `Code` and `Param` properties (added in 1.0.0-beta.3) are forwarded verbatim in the HTTP error response body, mirroring the `BadRequestException` API. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet test files are required for these changes ŌĆö the Troubleshooting and provider exception guidance does not need live code samples. Existing snippets remain valid. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections in order: 1. Getting started Ō£ģ (Install, Prerequisites, Configure the server) 2. Key concepts Ō£ģ 3. Examples Ō£ģ 4. Troubleshooting Ō£ģ ŌĆö needs the additions described in Step 1 5. Next steps Ō£ģ 6. Contributing Ō£ģ No structural changes are needed; only the content additions described in Step 1. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` ŌĆö confirm no README changes (no new snippet regions were added) 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` ŌĆö no public API changes are expected for these doc edits 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Firewall blocked 2 domains > > The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `pkgs.dev.azure.com` > - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "pkgs.dev.azure.com" > - "releaseassets.githubusercontent.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24653631953/agentic_workflow) ┬Ę ŌŚÅ 342K ┬Ę [ŌŚĘ](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58364","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:57Z","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:57Z"}

arguments.body:
## ­¤Ä» Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue documents gaps in `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` documentation following the beta.3 changes ŌĆö specifically missing coverage for eager `previous_response_id`/`conversation.id` validation, the new `Code`/`Param` properties on `ResourceNotFoundException`, and upstream error normalization behavior.

<details>
<summary>­¤ōŗ Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (1.0.0-beta.3)
**Affected API:** `ResponseContext.GetHistoryAsync()`, `ResourceNotFoundException`, `StorageErrorMapper`
**Scenarios:**
- Troubleshooting section missing 404 (eager `previous_response_id` validation) and 400 (`conversation.id` validation) error cases
- `ResponseContext.GetHistoryAsync()` description doesn't mention that provider validation happens eagerly before handler invocation
- `ResourceNotFoundException.Code` and `Param` properties (new in beta.3) undocumented in handler implementation guide
- No documentation of the guarantee that non-4xx upstream storage errors are normalized to HTTP 500

**Root ask:** Update `README.md` and `docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` to cover the behavioral changes introduced in beta.3.
</details>

<details>
<summary>­¤öÄ Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation gap issue (no code bug). Triggered by PR #58347.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Update `README.md` Troubleshooting section to add 404 (eager `previous_response_id`) and 400 (`conversation.id`) error bullets, and rename the existing 404 to specify get/cancel/delete context
2. Add a "Storage error propagation" note explaining HTTP 500 normalization for non-4xx upstream errors
3. Update `ResponseContext.GetHistoryAsync()` bullet to mention eager pre-handler validation and caching of resolved results
4. Update `docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` to document `ResourceNotFoundException.Code` and `Param` properties with example usage
</details>

<details>
<summary>­¤ÅĘ’ĖÅ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` ŌĆö documentation issue for a non-management SDK (`Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` does not start with `Azure.ResourceManager` or `Microsoft.Azure.Management`)
- **Service:** `AI Agents` ŌĆö issue explicitly references `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`, which maps to the `%AI Agents` CODEOWNERS PRLabel entry
- **Confidence:** High ŌĆö package name and service directory are explicitly stated; no ambiguity between services or categories
</details>

<details>
<summary>­¤æź Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Agents` (line 85) ŌĆö "AI Agents" is in the predicted labels; only this label is required by the entry
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @ankitbko @RaviPidaparthi
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; no assignment (ServiceOwners path, no AzureSdkOwners); "needs-team-attention" not added (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported)
- **Scan notes:** Scanned from bottom of CODEOWNERS (line 1228) upward; management/CodeGen entries near the bottom all require `%Mgmt` or `%CodeGen` labels not present on this issue ŌĆö skipped; first matching entry encountered was `%AI Agents` at line 85
</details>
